# My Web pages
by V.Zerkin, 2024

## JSON Viewer/Editor

### Purpose/Features/Links:
* Presenting **any** JSON text as interactive tree in order to 
  learn/understand/compare/discuss data structures/content/hierarchy
* Test samples: JSON files generated by nuclear data applications
  [EXFOR](https://nds.iaea.org/exfor/),
  [ENDF](https://nds.iaea.org/ends/),
  [IBANDL](https://nds.iaea.org/exfor/ibandl.htm),
  [Web-API](https://nds.iaea.org/exfor/x4guide/API/),
  [NSR](https://www.nndc.bnl.gov/nsr-dev/),
  ENSDF,
  etc.
* Viewer-part is extended to display specific information along with node title
* Editor implements operations: 
  * File: open/new/save
  * Node-json-text: modify/check/clear/copy/paste
  * Node-in-tree: move:up/down/remove
  * Edit: undo/redo
  * History: view history of operations roll back to previous steps
  * Tool: minify/expand/iTable current JSON text in pupup-window
* Application can be used in local Web-Browser without Web-server
* Edit-json-tree online:
  * vzerkin.github.io: [edit-json-tree](https://vzerkin.github.io/edit-json-tree/),
    [parallel-editing](https://vzerkin.github.io/edit-json-tree/cmp2json.htm)
  * zerkin.usite.pro: [edit-json-tree](https://zerkin.usite.pro/edit-json-tree/),
    [parallel-editing](https://vzerkin.github.io/edit-json-tree/cmp2json.htm)
